answer:
In 'The Ruin' (chapter 16 of 'Old English Elegies', slug: unknown-old-english-elegies-d5fe9e), the lines describing the bath houses with their hot streams are in paragraph p-dfdbda:

"Stone courts stood; a stream threw up heat / in a wide surge; a wall enclosed all / within its bright embrace, where the baths were, / hot at their heart. That was a welcome thing. / They let pour then over the grey stone / hot streams [...] / [...] until the round pool grew hot [...]"
